messages.content: 段3第三，非常微妙但非常重要:在增长有保证的时候决定安定下来。所以当你的生活一帆风顺的时候，你已经组建了一个很棒的团队，你的收入也在不断增长，一切都是固定的时间来安定下来。当我推出我的第一本书时，我非常非常努力地在巴西各地发行它。就这样，超过三百万人下载了它，超过五万人购买了实体书。当我写续集的时候，一定会有一定的影响。即使我做得很少，销量也还可以。但还好，绝不是还好。当你朝着一个高峰成长时，你需要比以往任何时候都更加努力，为自己找到另一个高峰。对吧?也许如果我不做什么，几十万人就会读到它，这已经很棒了。但如果我比以往更加努力，我可以把这个数字提高到数百万。这就是为什么我决定，带着我的新书，去巴西的每一个州。我已经看到了一个更高的高峰。没有时间静下心来。用英语概括一下上面这段话

When your life is going well and your income is increasing, it's important to stabilize and not take it for granted. Even if you have guaranteed growth, it's crucial to work harder than ever to find another peak. The author's first book sold well, so they plan to tour every state in Brazil with their new book to reach an even higher number of readers. The key is to never become complacent and always push for more success.
翻译一下
请您提供需要翻译的内容。

When your life is going well and your income is increasing, it’s important to stabilize and not take it for granted. Even if you have guaranteed growth, it’s crucial to work harder than ever to find another peak. The author’s first book sold well, so they plan to tour every state in Brazil with their new book to reach an even higher number of readers. The key is to never become complacent and always push for more success.
当你的生活一帆风顺，你的收入也在不断增长时，稳定自己的生活，不把这种状况视为理所当然是很重要的。即使你的增长有保证，仍然要比以往任何时候都更加努力，为自己找到另一个高峰。作者的第一本书销售得不错，所以计划在巴西每一个州都进行新书的巡回宣传，以吸引更多读者。关键是永远不要自满，不断追求更多的成功。

Two: Believe someone else has the answers for you. Constantly, people want to help out, right? All sorts of people: your family, your friends, your business partners, they all have opinions on which path you should take: “And let me tell you, go through this pipe.” But whenever you go inside, there are other ways you have to pick as well. And you need to make those decisions yourself. No one else has the perfect answers for your life. And you need to keep picking those decisions, right? The pipes are infinite and you’re going to bump your head, and it’s a part of the process.概括一下

The author advises against believing that others have all the answers for your life. People may try to give you advice and guide you, but ultimately you must make your own decisions. No one has all the perfect answers for your life and you must keep making decisions, even if it means making mistakes and learning along the way.

Segment 1
I dedicated the past two years to understanding how people achieve their dreams. When we think about the dreams we have, and the dent
 we want to leave in the universe, it is striking to see how big of an overlap there is between the dreams that we have and projects that never happen. (Laughter) So I’m here to talk to you today about five ways how not to follow your dreams.
One: Believe in overnight success. You know the story, right? The tech guy built a mobile app and sold it very fast for a lot of money. You know, the story may seem real, but I bet it’s incomplete. If you go investigate further, the guy has done 30 apps before and he has done a Master’s on the topic, a Ph.D. He has been working on the topic for like 20 years.
This is really interesting, I myself have a story in Brazil that people think is an overnight success. I come from a humble family, and two weeks before the deadline to apply to MIT, I started the application process. And, voila! I got in. People may think it’s an overnight success, but that only worked because for the 17 years prior to that, I took life and education seriously. Your overnight success story is always a result of everything you’ve done in your life through that moment.

作者花了两年时间研究人们如何实现自己的梦想。很多人会拥有各种各样的梦想，但很多都无法实现。作者会在此分享五种方法，告诉大家如何不要去实现自己的梦想。第一种方法是相信“一夜暴富”的成功。常听到这样的故事，一位技术男实现了一款移动应用的成功并迅速卖出获得了大量资金，但这样的故事通常是不完整的。实际上，这位技术男之前已经创办了30款应用，并且有硕士和博士学位，已经从事这个领域超过20年了。作者自己也会有被认为是一夜成功的故事，但其实能取得成功是因为前17年他一直认真对待生活和学习。不管是谁的一夜之间的成功故事，都是他们在之前的努力和积累的结果。

I dedicated the past two years to understanding how people achieve their dreams. When we think about the dreams we have, and the dent
we want to leave in the universe, it is striking to see how big of an overlap there is between the dreams that we have and projects that never happen. (Laughter) So I’m here to talk to you today about five ways how not to follow your dreams.
作者花了过去两年时间去研究人们如何实现自己的梦想。当我们想到自己的梦想和想要在这个世界上留下的痕迹时，发现我们的梦想和最终未能实现的项目之间有很大的重叠部分。所以，今天我来跟大家分享五种不要去实现自己梦想的方法。

One: Believe in overnight success. You know the story, right? The tech guy built a mobile app and sold it very fast for a lot of money. You know, the story may seem real, but I bet it’s incomplete. If you go investigate further, the guy has done 30 apps before and he has done a Master’s on the topic, a Ph.D. He has been working on the topic for like 20 years.
This is really interesting, I myself have a story in Brazil that people think is an overnight success. I come from a humble family, and two weeks before the deadline to apply to MIT, I started the application process. And, voila! I got in. People may think it’s an overnight

成功的故事。但其实这背后有一个不完整的故事。如果你进一步调查，你会发现这位技术男之前已经创办了30款应用，并且有硕士和博士学位，已经从事这个领域超过20年了。这真的很有趣。其实我也有一个在巴西被认为是一夜成名的故事。我来自一个不起眼的家庭，在MIT的申请截止日期前两周，我开始了申请流程。然后，神奇的事情发生了，我被录取了。人们可能觉得这是一夜之间的成功故事，但其实这背后是我前17年对生活和学习的认真对待。不管是谁的一夜之间的成功故事，都是他们在之前的努力和积累的结果。

I dedicated the past two years to understanding how people achieve their dreams. When we think about the dreams we have, and the dent
we want to leave in the universe, it is striking to see how big of an overlap there is between the dreams that we have and projects that never happen. (Laughter) So I’m here to talk to you today about five ways how not to follow your dreams.One: Believe in overnight success. You know the story, right? The tech guy built a mobile app and sold it very fast for a lot of money. You know, the story may seem real, but I bet it’s incomplete. If you go investigate further, the guy has done 30 apps before and he has done a Master’s on the topic, a Ph.D. He has been working on the topic for like 20 years.
This is really interesting, I myself have a story in Brazil that people think is an overnight success. I come from a humble family, and two weeks before the deadline to apply to MIT, I started the application process. And, voila! I got in. People may think it’s an overnight success, but that only worked because for the 17 years prior to that, I took life and education seriously. Your overnight success story is always a result of everything you’ve done in your life through that moment.概括一下

作者花了两年时间去研究人们如何实现自己的梦想。我们想到的梦想和想留下的印记，与最终未能成功的项目之间有很大的重叠。因此，作者在这里跟大家分享了五种不要去实现自己梦想的方法。其中之一是相信一夜成功。虽然我们常听到一些成功故事，但其实这些故事往往是不完整的。要取得成功，往往需要长时间的努力和积累。就像作者自己在巴西的申请到MIT，人们可能认为是一夜成名，但其实这背后是作者前17年的努力和认真对待。因此，任何形式的一夜成功都是我们在之前的努力和积累基础上的结果。
用英语概括

The author spent two years researching how people achieve their dreams and noticed a significant overlap between their dreams and projects that never happen. The author discusses five ways not to follow your dreams, one of which is believing in overnight success. Success often requires long periods of hard work and dedication, and any form of overnight success is the result of prior effort and accumulation.

Fourth tip, and that’s really important: Believe the fault is someone else’s. I constantly see people saying, “Yes, I had this great idea, but no investor had the vision to invest.” “Oh, I created this great product, but you know the market is so bad, the sales didn’t go well.” Or, “I can’t find good talent; my team is so below expectations.” If you have dreams, it’s your responsibility to make them happen. Yes, it may be hard to find talent. Yes, the market may be bad. But if no one invested in your idea, if no one bought your product, for sure, there is something there that is your fault. (Laughter) Definitely.

You need to get your dreams and make them happen. And no one achieved their goals alone. But if you didn’t make them happen, it’s your fault and no one else’s. Be responsible for your dreams. 用英语概括一下

The fourth tip is to take responsibility for your dreams and not blame others for their failure. Many people make excuses for not achieving their dreams, such as a lack of investment or bad market conditions, but it's up to you to make them happen. No one achieved their goals alone, but if you don't make them happen, it's your own fault and responsibility. Be accountable for your dreams.

And one last tip, and this one is really important as well: Believe that the only things that matter are the dreams themselves. Once I saw an ad, and it was a lot of friends, they were going up a mountain, it was a very high mountain, and it was a lot of work. You could see that they were sweating and this was tough. And they were going up, and they finally made it to the peak. Of course, they decided to celebrate, right? I’m going to celebrate, so, “Yes! We made it, we’re at the top!” Two seconds later, one looks at the other and says, “Okay, let’s go down.” (Laughter)

Life is never about the goals themselves. Life is about the journey. Yes, you should enjoy the goals themselves, but people think that you have dreams, and whenever you get to reaching one of those dreams, it’s a magical place where happiness will be all around. But achieving a dream is a momentary sensation, and your life is not. The only way to really achieve all of your dreams is to fully enjoy every step of your journey. That’s the best way.And your journey is simple—it’s made of steps. Some steps will be right on. Sometimes you will trip. If it’s right on, celebrate, because some people wait a lot to celebrate. And if you tripped, turn that into something to learn. If every step becomes something to learn or something to celebrate, you will for sure enjoy the journey.

So, five tips: Believe in overnight success, believe someone else has the answers for you, believe that when growth is guaranteed, you should settle down, believe the fault is someone else’s, and believe that only the goals themselves matter. Believe me, if you do that, you will destroy your dreams. (Laughter) Thank you.用英语概括一下

The final tip is to believe that only the attainment of your dreams matters, rather than the journey. The speaker emphasizes the importance of enjoying every step of your journey and turning setbacks into learning experiences. Celebrate each success along the way and take responsibility for your own dreams. The five tips to avoid destroying your dreams are to not believe in overnight success, not rely on others for answers, not settle down when growth is guaranteed and not blame others for failure. Overall, it's important to embrace the journey towards achieving your dreams and celebrate every step of the way.
翻译一下上面这段话
这个部分的提示是相信只有实现梦想本身才真正重要，而不是旅途本身。演讲者强调了享受整个旅程的重要性，并将挫折转化为学习经验。要庆祝旅途中的每一个成功，并对自己的梦想负责。避免摧毁你的梦想的五个提示是不要相信一夜成名，不要信任他人的答案，不要因为有保障的增长而安定下来，不要因为失败而责怪别人，以及相信只有梦想本身才重要。总的来说，重要的是拥抱实现你的梦想的旅程，并庆祝每一步的成功。
